ground_truth.title: Strategist or Futurist: The Crossroads Defining Physical Security's Next Era
ground_truth.author: Jeffrey A. Slotnick CPP, PSP
ground_truth.main_content: From PSIM's rise and fall to the emergence of integration alliances, connected intelligence, and agentic AI, the physical security industry stands at a critical inflection point. Twenty-six years ago, network video was closed-circuit television (CCTV) and a standalone system. H.264 video technology, officially released in 2003, became the industry standard for video compression due to its ability to deliver high-quality video while significantly reducing file size. The concept of Physical Security Information Management (PSIM) was introduced in 2006, marking a pivotal shift in how information from previously isolated security systems could be unified into a single platform. However, PSIM platforms functioned primarily as middleware, limiting their overall effectiveness and scalability. PSIMS were proprietary systems, many early security technologies were closed and difficult to integrate. PSIMS often failed to deliver a truly unified interface, leading to user frustration. Key open architecture initiatives include OSDP, ONVIF, PSIA, MQTT, and SNMP, though none has resulted in a universally accepted open architecture. Following the decline of PSIM platforms, the industry took an unexpected turn with the emergence of alliances among like-minded manufacturers who align around a shared architecture to expand market presence. These alliances represent collaborative efforts among industry leaders such as Honeywell, Sony, Genetec, and Lenel, reshaping how organizations protect their people and assets. APIs and Web Parts are transforming how physical security systems are integrated and managed. Are you a Strategist or a Futurist? A strategist focuses on current realities and near-term planning, while a futurist explores long-term possibilities and emerging disruptions. In the Fifth Industrial Revolution, strategists will build the bridge, and futurists will chart the destination. Despite rapid technological advancement, many current financial incentives continue to reinforce the status quo. To truly embrace the future, our industry must adopt open architecture, break down silos, and align with the principles of Connected Intelligence and Agentic AI. Agentic AI is a class of artificial intelligence systems designed to act autonomously and purposefully to achieve specific goals with minimal human supervision.
with: The concept of Physical Security Information Management (PSIM) was introduced to the security industry in 2006, marking a pivotal shift in how information from previously isolated security systems could be unified into a single platform.

Following the decline of PSIM platforms, it was anticipated that there would be a natural progression toward open-architecture solutions, but the industry once again took an unexpected turn with the emergence of what I've come to call The Alliances.
APIs, or Application Programming Interfaces, are foundational to modern physical security integration, serving as structured bridges between different software systems.
Are you a Strategist or a Futurist? A strategist focuses on current realities and near-term planning, while a futurist explores long-term possibilities and emerging disruptions.
To truly embrace the future, our industry must adopt open architecture, break down silos, and align with the principles of Connected Intelligence and Agentic AI.
